summary: Frontend-only end-to-end testing of KOC Season 2 PWA iteration-2 (Schedule/Matchups/More restoration + 5-tab BottomNav restructure) on mobile viewport 390x844 against live preview URL. All 14 review-request scenarios pass. Bottom nav renders fixed at bottom with exactly 5 tabs (Teams/Schedule/Standings/Matchups/More) totaling 390px width with no overflow; active tab highlight works on tap. Schedule page renders all 9 week cards with the Thanksgiving Break card between weeks 6 and 7, Friday matches show blue accent + 'FRI' badge, Saturday show orange + 'SAT', each match shows day/time/both teams with captain names in parens and a 'vs' separator, and each week shows a Bye team banner. Team filter (Rudra Racquets) correctly narrows each week to a single RR match (weeks 2-9 went from 4→1 matches each), week 1 collapses to only the RR bye (0 matches + bye card), week-2 bye (Karna's Crusaders) is correctly hidden when filter=RR, Clear-filter button restores all weeks. Playoffs section renders 4 cards with Championship Final correctly tagged. Matchups page mounts with 3 tabs; Player Stats is default tab; each tab has its own search input and renders the correct empty-state row ('No player data yet' / 'No player data yet' / 'No doubles data yet'). More page is role-aware: guest sees Match History + Rules + Captain/Admin Login (no logout); after admin login (KOCPO#ADMIN) it shows History+Rules+Enter Score+Admin Dashboard+Log out (no Login link); after team-captain login (Spin Kings / KOCSK#2) it shows History+Rules+Enter Score (with 'Logged in as Spin Kings' desc)+Log out (no Admin link). Tapping each More item navigates to the correct route (/history, /rules, /score, /admin, /login). Logout clears session and Login reappears. ProtectedTeam/ProtectedAdmin guards still redirect guests from /score and /admin to /login. Regression: Teams page renders all 16 team cards (team-card-RR..T16), Standings shows 17 table rows (header+16 teams). No horizontal scroll on 390px viewport (scrollWidth=clientWidth=390).
action_items: (Optional, carry-over from iter_1) Suppress React Router v6 future-flag console warnings by opting in (v7_startTransition, v7_relativeSplatPath).
critical_code_review_comments: Matchups.js computeStats() falls back to match.t1/match.t2 for player team mapping when player not found in any team roster, but match objects from ScoreEntry don't seem to set t1/t2 at the top level (it uses lines[].players.team1/team2). If a player name is mistyped in a match but matches a roster, they get the team; otherwise team will be 'Unknown'. Acceptable, but worth confirming with main agent if this matters.

scheduleData.js: schedule is hard-coded with team names + captain names. If admin renames a team via Admin Dashboard, the Schedule page will continue to show the old name (no Firebase coupling). Consider noting this in deployment docs or deriving from teams in DB.
More.js item.testid for the same Link key uses item.to as React key. If two items shared the same path it would collide — fine today since each item has a unique route.

Schedule.js filter logic: when a non-bye team is filtered, weeks where they're neither playing nor on bye correctly hide; verified working. Minor: 'Clear filter' button could also be a dedicated reset on the select rather than a separate button, but current UX is fine.
test_credentials: Admin: KOCPO#ADMIN. Team captain example used: Spin Kings (KOCSK#2). Full list at /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: None. No Firebase writes performed in this iteration (admin/team logins read-only, no score submissions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-2 covered the new Schedule/Matchups/More pages and the 5-tab BottomNav. Routes /schedule, /matchups, /more, /history, /rules all reachable from the bottom nav and More menu. ProtectedTeam/ProtectedAdmin still gate /score and /admin. Matchups tabs all render empty-state when no matches exist in Firebase — to test populated state, submit a match via /score (admin or captain) then revisit /matchups; computeStats() expects match.lines[].players.team1/team2, line.g1/g2, line.type ('singles'|'doubles'). data-testid coverage on new pages is complete and matches the spec in review request 'other_misc_info'. The schedule is hard-coded in src/data/scheduleData.js — not loaded from Firebase, so team rename in admin won't update it.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found in this iteration.
